Items.Title: 我写了 1000 篇文章，下一步是什么？ | I wrote 1000 articles, what’s next?
Items.Description:
你们中的大多数人都知道我的一致性，这是我博客系列中的金箭。

我在 1008 天内写了 1000 篇文章！
几乎每天一篇文章，我的蜜月是我度过的唯一假期。

我为这一成就感到非常自豪； 这是一段美妙的旅程。

但下一步是什么？

我花了一段时间才考虑到这一点。
我要继续每天写作吗？ 我想尝试其他事情吗？ 下一件大事是什么？

在回答这个问题之前，我需要公开一个事实，那就是每日写作是非常独特的，我喜欢它，但在写到第 1000 篇文章时也很累。

然后一些神奇而令人兴奋的事情改变了我对一切的整个想法。

我们怀孕了！
太棒了，我准备好当爸爸了。

期待什么

那么好吧，未来对我有什么期望？
我永远不会停止创作内容，因为我非常喜欢它，但我决定远离日常内容。

有几个方面我想尝试找到下一个最好的东西：

  • 长篇文章
  • 电子书

在这些之间，我会在每个内容片段上投入更多时间，甚至可能会更深入地研究我已经创建的一些内容。

除此之外，我还有一些我想要执行的爱好项目和想法，所以请留意这些。

网站呢？

好吧，我可能会在我认为合适的时候不定期地发布一篇文章，但事实是，我正计划出售该网站。

它已经成为我的宝贝将近三年了，它已经成熟了很多，我很乐意看到有人接管它并继续为新开发者提供独特的内容。

如果那个人是你，请务必联系 🙏。

一些数字

你们中的许多人可能来这里是为了看一些数字，对吧？
以下是其中一些：

  • 每月 160K+ 页面浏览量
  • 平均每天 10 万次展示
  • 仅搜索字词就有 5000 次点击
  • 还有更多

我高兴极了。
一开始，我从没想过这是可能的，但我做到了。

这不是因为我的写作，而是因为你们这些了不起的人激励着我并阅读了我的文章。

所以从心底里：

感谢您加入我的旅程。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系起来！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 欢迎订阅我的电子邮件时事通讯并连接Facebook或Twitter



原文:

Most of you know me for my consistency, a golden arrow in my blog series.

I've written 1000 articles in 1008 days!
Almost an article a day, and my honeymoon was the only holiday I ever took.

I'm super proud of this achievement; it has been a fantastic journey.

But what's next?

And it took me a while to think about that.
Do I want to continue writing daily? Do I want to try out other things? What is the next big thing?

And before answering that, I need to open up about the fact that writing daily is super unique, and I loved it, but it was also quite draining towards the 1000th article.

And then something magical and exciting changed my whole thinking about everything.

We got pregnant!
So amazing, and I'm ready to become a dad.

What to expect

So alright, what to expect from me in the future?
I will never stop creating content as I enjoy it so much, but I decided to move away from daily content.

There are a couple of aspects I want to try to find the next best thing:

  • Youtube videos
  • Long-form articles
  • Ebooks

Between those, I'll dedicate more time per content piece and might even go deeper into some of the ones I already created.

Besides that, I have a few hobby projects and ideas I want to execute, so keep an eye out for those.

What about the site?

Well, I might drop an article irregularly as I see fit, but the truth is, I'm planning on selling the website.

It's been my baby for almost three years, and it has matured a lot to a point where I'd love to see someone take it over and keep providing unique content for new developers.

If that person is you, please do get in touch 🙏.

Some numbers

Many of you probably came here to see some numbers, right?
What do 1000 articles represent?

And here are some of them:

  • 528704 words
  • 1920 newsletter subscribers
  • 160K+ page views every month
  • 110+ #1 ranked google queries
  • an average of 100K impressions daily
  • 5K clicks on search terms alone
  • And so much more

I couldn't be happier.
In the beginning, I'd never thought that would even be possible, yet I achieved it.

And that's not because of my writing, but because of all you amazing people motivating me and reading the articles.

So from the bottom of my heart:

Thank you for joining me on this journey.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Items.Title: 区块链技术简介 | INTRODUCTION TO BLOCKCHAIN TECHNOLOGY
Items.Description:
去中心化不是一个新概念，而是构建产品或任何技术解决方案时的主要架构之一。 由于网络是公开透明的，所有交易都记录在公共分类账上，去中心化提供了更大的透明度和安全性。 这允许任何人查看和验证网络交易，这有助于确保系统的完整性。

总的来说，去中心化是区块链技术的一个重要方面，它有助于网络的安全性、透明度和完整性。

在区块链技术中，去中心化是必不可少的，因为它赋予用户对数据的控制权，并允许他们在没有第三方许可的情况下做出决定。 此外，去中心化是将控制权从集中式系统（个人、组织或团体）转移到分布式网络。
在本文中，我们将重点关注区块链技术，重点关注以下主题：

什么是区块链技术？

区块链技术是一种网络系统，将交易记录、凭证和其他详细信息等信息存储在不同的计算机网络上，并且不允许第三方更改。

区块链中的分类帐类似于数据库，其中保存了所有活动。 区块链分类帐由不同的块组成，该块维护所有信息和记录。

现在，让我们熟悉一下区块链技术的目的。

区块链技术的目的是什么？

区块链技术的目的； 如下所述
允许记录和分发数字信息。

  • 结束数据操作。
  • 灌输数据监管的透明度。
  • 记录将保持不变的交易。
  • 建立信任、释放价值等。

知道了这一点，为了充分了解，让我们看看不同类型的区块链技术。

区块链技术有哪些不同类型？

在区块链技术中，我们有四 (4) 种类型的区块链，它们是

  1. 公共区块链
  2. 私人区块链
  3. 混合区块链
  4. 联盟区块链

现在，让我们更详细地了解上面列出的类型。

公共区块链

公共区块链经常用于创建安全透明的交易记录，例如财务或供应链记录。
让我们看一个公共区块链的例子，这包括比特币、以太坊、 莱特币、达世币和Zcash区块链。 这些区块链中的每一个都有其独特的功能和用途，但它们都向公众开放并允许任何人参与。

私人区块链

私有区块链的一个例子可能是一家公司使用区块链来跟踪其供应链中货物的流动。 该公司可以建立一个私有区块链网络，并向其合作伙伴（如供应商和客户）授予访问权限，使他们能够跟踪货物的流动并确保供应链的完整性。

混合区块链

混合区块链是由单一组织拥有的区块链网络，共享私有和公共区块链属性，这允许私有组织开发与基于公共系统（无许可）相关的基于许可的系统。

这是一个混合区块链的例子：

考虑一个供应链管理系统，许多企业使用它来监控产品从制造商到零售商的流动。 混合区块链可用于构建该系统，使供应链参与者能够访问区块链的私有部分，以便他们可以添加、编辑和确认有关物品移动的数据。 然而，通过使客户、监管机构和其他利益相关者能够在无法编辑的情况下检查有关货物流动的信息，区块链的公共部分可用于为他们带来透明度。 通过这样做，企业可以继续让外部各方访问其内部流程和数据，同时保护他们的隐私。

联盟区块链。

联盟区块链是一种特殊类型的区块链网络，由多家公司而非一家公司运营。 当参与的公司希望拥有一定程度的网络控制权和决定网络如何运行的权力时，通常会采用这种区块链。

马可波罗网络是由银行、贸易融资提供商和技术公司组成的财团运营的贸易融资平台，是财团区块链的另一个例子。 参与组织可以为平台的创建和管理做出贡献，该平台旨在自动化和简化贸易融资程序。

此外，联盟区块链对于希望在单一区块链平台上协同工作同时对网络增长有一定程度控制的企业来说是一个有用的工具。

区块链技术的使用

在区块链技术的帮助下，多方可以安全地以透明、不可更改和安全的方式保存和记录交易。 它经常被用来制作交易的数字分类账，可用于追踪资产流向、确认文件的合法性或分发数据存储。

比特币等加密货币的发展是区块链技术最著名的用途之一。 区块链在这个场景中被用来监控数字货币从一个人到另一个人的转移，并确认交易的合法性。

区块链技术的其他潜在用途包括

金融机构：区块链技术可用于构建安全、开放的金融机构，如比特币交易所、数字钱包等。

供应链管理：通过跟踪供应链中的物品流动，区块链技术可以确保产品的采购有效且合乎道德。

身份验证：使用区块链技术，可以使数字身份安全且可验证。 然后，这些身份可用于从护照和投票系统验证到在线投票的所有事情。

智能合约：使用区块链技术，创建在满足特定条件时生效的自动执行合约，从而实现复杂法律安排的自动化。

数据管理：区块链技术可用于构建透明、安全的系统来存储和交换敏感数据，包括学术或医疗记录。

总的来说，区块链技术有可能通过促进安全、开放和有效的数据管理和交易流程来改变各种行业。

现在让我们看一下区块链技术和加密货币之间的比较。

区块链技术与加密货币

根据cirusfoundation ，以下是差异：

序列号区块链技术加密货币
区块链技术是一个分散的平台。 加密货币是去中心化资产。
区块链可以是公共的也可以是私有的。 加密货币主要是公开的。
区块链是不可变的。 加密货币不是。
区块链速度慢且可扩展性有限。 加密货币速度很快，并且具有无限可扩展性的潜力。
区块链是一个分布式数据库，允许安全、透明和防篡改的记录保存。 加密货币是一种使用加密技术确保安全的数字资产。

现在您对区块链技术有了很好的了解。 让我们看看一些您可以访问的好平台，以了解有关新兴技术的更多信息。

是否有任何区块链免费学习资源？

当然，为了回答您的问题，以下是您可以随时查看并保持更新的资源位置：

上述平台经过精心挑选，供您使用，为您全面学习区块链技术的旅程提供一个良好的开端。

结论
区块链技术是一种分布式账本，一种开源技术，允许任何人构建智能合约应用程序：并且还允许商业圈内共享的信息透明化。

话虽如此，我们已经能够涵盖“区块链技术”的基本方面，其中我们解释了区块链技术的全部内容和区块链技术的目的，并且还共享资源，您可以在其中了解更多有关新技术的信息.

最后，通过学习和保持更新来利用新技术对您来说非常好。

谢谢阅读。



原文:

Decentralization is not a new concept but one of the primary architectures when building a product or any technology solution. Because the network is open and transparent, and all transactions are recorded on a public ledger, decentralization provides greater transparency and security. This allows anyone to view and verify network transactions, which helps to ensure the system's integrity.

Overall, decentralization is an important aspect of blockchain technology that contributes to the network's security, transparency, and integrity.

In blockchain technology, decentralization is essential, because it gives users control over data, and allows them to make decisions without the permission of a third party. Moreover, decentralization is the removal of control from a centralized system (individual, organization, or group) to a distributed network.
In this article, We will center our focus on Blockchain Technology, where the concentration will be on the subject below:

What is Blockchain Technology?

Blockchain Technology is a network system that stores information such as transaction records, credentials, and other details on a different computer network and doesn’t allow changes from a third party.

A ledger in blockchain is similar to a database, where all the activities are kept. The blockchain ledger is made up of different blocks and this block maintains all information and records.

Now, let's get acquainted with the purpose of Blockchain Technology.

What is the Purpose of Blockchain Technology?

The Purpose of Blockchain Technology; is stated below
To allow digital information to be recorded and distributed.

  • To end data manipulation.
  • To instill transparency over data regulation.
  • To record transactions that will remain unchanged.
  • To build trust, unlock value, etc.

Having known that, to be fully knowledgeable, let's look at the different types of Blockchain Technology.

What are the different types of Blockchain Technology?

In Blockchain Technology, we have four (4) types of Blockchains, and these are

  1. Public Blockchains
  2. Private Blockchains
  3. Hybrid Blockchains
  4. Consortium Blockchains

Now, let's go into more detail with the above-listed types.

Public Blockchains

A public blockchain is an open, decentralized distributed ledger in which anyone can participate as a user or validator. Public blockchains are frequently used to create a secure and transparent record of transactions, such as financial or supply chain records.
Let's see an example of a public blockchain, and this includes Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, Dash, and the Zcash blockchain. Each of these blockchains has its own unique features and uses, but all of them are open to the public and allow anyone to participate.

Private Blockchains

A private Blockchain is a blockchain network owned by external bodies such as an organization that only grants access to users through invitation, and every user is validated before entries.
An example of a private blockchain could be a company that uses a blockchain to track the movement of goods within its supply chain. The company could set up a private blockchain network and grant access to its partners, such as suppliers and customers, to enable them to track the movement of goods and ensure the integrity of the supply chain.

Hybrid Blockchains

A hybrid blockchain is a blockchain network owned by a single organization that shared both private and public blockchain attributes, this allows private organizations to develop a permission-based system related to the public-based system (permissionless).

Here is an example of a hybrid blockchain:

Consider a supply chain management system that a number of businesses use to monitor the flow of products from the manufacturer to the retailer. A hybrid blockchain might be used to build this system, giving supply chain participants access to the blockchain's private section so they can add, edit, and confirm data regarding the movement of items. However, by enabling customers, regulators, and other stakeholders to examine the information about the movement of goods without being able to edit it, the public section of the blockchain can be used to bring transparency to them. By doing this, businesses may continue to give external parties access to their internal processes and data while maintaining their privacy.

Consortium Blockchains.

A consortium blockchain is a particular kind of blockchain network that is run by several firms as opposed to just one. When participating companies wish to have some degree of network control and the power to decide how the network is run, this kind of blockchain is often employed.

The Marco Polo network, a trade finance platform run by a consortium of banks, trade finance providers, and technology firms, is another example of a consortium blockchain. The participating organizations can contribute to the creation and management of the platform, which is aimed to automate and streamline trade finance procedures.

Moreover, consortium blockchains are a helpful tool for businesses who want to work together on a single blockchain platform while yet having some degree of control over the network's growth.

Use of Blockchain Technology

With the help of blockchain technology, numerous parties may safely keep and record transactions in a way that is transparent, unchangeable, and secure. It is frequently employed to produce a digital ledger of transactions, which can be used to trace the flow of assets, confirm the legitimacy of papers, or distribute data storage.

The development of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin is one of the best-known uses of blockchain technology. The blockchain is used in this scenario to monitor the transfer of digital currency from one person to another and to confirm the legitimacy of transactions.

Other potential uses for blockchain technology include

Financial Institutions: Blockchain technology can be used to build safe and open financial institutions, such as bitcoin exchanges and digital wallets.

Supply chain management: By tracking the flow of items across a supply chain, blockchain technology can make sure that products are sourced effectively and ethically.

Identity verification: Using blockchain technology, digital identities may be made secure and verifiable. These identities can then be utilized for everything from passport and voting system verification to online voting.

Smart contracts: Using blockchain technology, self-executing contracts that take effect when certain criteria are met are created, allowing for the automation of intricate legal arrangements.

Data management: Blockchain technology can be used to build transparent, safe systems for storing and exchanging sensitive data, including academic or medical records.

Overall, blockchain technology has the potential to change a variety of industries by facilitating safe, open, and effective data management and transaction processes.

Let’s now look at the comparison between Blockchain Technology and Cryptocurrency.

Blockchain Technology vs Cryptocurrency

According to cirusfoundation, below are the differences:

SN Blockchain Technology Cryptocurrency
1 Blockchain Technology is a decentralized platform. Cryptocurrency is decentralized asset.
2 Blockchain can be public or private. Cryptocurrency is mostly public.
3 Blockchain is immutable. Cryptocurrency is not.
4 Blockchain is slow and has limited scalability. Cryptocurrency is fast and has the potential for infinite scalability.
5 Blockchain is a distributed database that allows for secure, transparent, and tamper-proof record-keeping. Cryptocurrency is a digital asset that uses cryptography for security.

Now that you have a good understanding of Blockchain Technology. Let’s see some good platforms you can visit to learn more about emerging technology.

Are there any Blockchain free resources for learning?

Sure, to answer your question, below are resource places you can always look up to and stay updated:

The above platform has been carefully selected for your consumption and to give you a good start in your journey to learn Blockchain Technology at large.

Conclusion
Blockchain Technology is a distributed ledger, an open-source technology that allows anyone to build Smart contract applications: and also allows the transparency of information shared within the business circle.

That being said, we have been able to cover the basic aspect of “Blockchain Technology” where we explained what blockchain technology is all about and the purpose of blockchain technology, and also share resources, where you can learn a lot more about the new technology.

Finally, it will be great for you to take advantage of the new technology through learning and staying updated.

Thanks for reading.

Items.Title: 如何使用矩阵解码密码 | How to decode a cryptogram using matrices
Items.Description:
发布于 12 月 24 日

您是否遇到过密码并想知道如何对其进行解码？ 如果是这样，你很幸运！ 今天我们讨论“如何使用矩阵解码密码？” 使用矩阵解码密码引述答案是一种简单而有效的方法，可以帮助您破解任何代码。 通过了解矩阵的基础知识及其工作原理，您可以轻松破译任何密码。 在这篇博文中，我们将具体讨论如何做到这一点。 今天的密码引述答案

什么是密码？
密码是一种谜题，其中通过将每个字母替换为另一个字母来对短语或句子进行加密。 目标是通过重新排列密码中的字母并用正确的字母替换它们来解码隐藏的消息，通常是引语或谚语。 为此，必须熟悉加密技术，例如替代密码、换位密码和其他代码。

解码密码的一种方法是使用矩阵。 矩阵是可用于以紧凑形式表示数据的数学对象。 当应用于密码时，矩阵可用于为密码的每个位置确定最可能的字母。 要使用矩阵解码密码，您需要：

  1. 将密码分成许多行和列。

  2. 用原始字母表的字母频率计数填充矩阵。

  3. 求解矩阵中的线性方程以找到最可能的解。

  4. 确定哪些字母对应于哪些列，然后将这些字母插入密码中。

  5. 重复直到密码被解决。

使用矩阵求解密码是快速解码消息的有效方法。 通过实践，可以精通用矩阵解码密码。

Cryptoquote Answer

Posted on Dec 24

Have you ever encountered a cryptogram and wondered how to decode it? If so, you’re in luck! Today we’re discussing ‘How do you decode a cryptogram using matrices?’ Using matrices to decode a cryptoquote answer is a simple yet effective method that can help you crack any code. By understanding the basics of matrices and how they work, you can easily decipher any cryptogram. In this blog post, we’ll discuss exactly how to do that.todays cryptoquote answer

What is a cryptogram?
A cryptogram is a type of puzzle where a short phrase or sentence is encrypted by replacing each letter with another letter. The goal is to decode the hidden message, usually a quote or proverb, by rearranging the letters in the cryptogram and substituting them with the correct letters. To do this, one must be familiar with encryption techniques such as substitution ciphers, transposition ciphers, and other codes.

One method for decoding a cryptogram is to use matrices. Matrices are mathematical objects that can be used to represent data in a compact form. When applied to cryptograms, matrices can be used to determine the most likely letter for each position of the cryptogram. To decode a cryptogram using matrices, you need to:

  1. Divide the cryptogram into a number of rows and columns.

  2. Fill in the matrix with letter frequency counts of the original alphabet.

  3. Solve the linear equations in the matrix to find the most likely solution.

  4. Determine which letters correspond to which columns, then plug these letters into the cryptogram.

  5. Repeat until the cryptogram is solved.

Using matrices to solve cryptograms is an efficient way to quickly decode messages. With practice, it is possible to become proficient at decoding cryptograms with matrices.

原文:

Zaw Htut Win

Posted on Dec 24

JPA JPA ဆိုတာ standard တစ်ခုဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ JPA ကို အခြေခံပြီး Hibernate, Eclipse-Link အစရှိသဖြင့် implementation လုပ်ကြပါတယ်။ အဲဒီထဲမှာ အသုံးအများဆုံးက Hibernate implementation ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဥပမာ HTML language ဆိုတာ standard ဖြစ်ပြီး သူ့ကို render လုပ်တဲ့ browser implementation တွေမတူကြသလိုပါပဲ။ ဥပမာ Firefox, Chrome, Edge အစရှိသဖြင့်ပေါ့။ JPA ကိုပြန်ပြောရမယ်ဆိုရင်တော့ JPA entity ဆိုတာ database table ကို ကိုယ်စားပြုတဲ့ Java class ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

အဲဒီ class ရဲ့ field တွေကတော့ database table ရဲ့ column တွေကို ကိုယ်စားပြုပါတယ်။ ပြီးရင် example နဲ့တကွပြပါ့မယ်။

Hibernate Hibernate ဟာ JPA implementation တွေထဲမှာ ပေါ်ပြူလာအဖြစ်ဆုံး ဖြစ်ပြီး Spring Boot က သူ့ကို default အနေနဲ့ ထည့်ထားပေးပါတယ်။ Hibernate ဟာ mature ဖြစ်နေပြီ

ဖြစ်တဲ့အပြင် large-scale application ကြီးတွေမှာ ကို သုံးကြပါတယ်။

Entity class များ create လုပ်ခြင်း Entity class class ဆိုတာ JPA ရဲ့ @Entity ဆိုတဲ့ annotation ကို class definition မှာရေးထားတဲ့ class ကိုခေါ်ပါတယ်။

Entity class မှာ getter နဲ့ setter method တွေပါရမှာဖြစ်ပြီး။ သူ့ရဲ့ field တွေကိုတော့ private ကြေငြာထားရပါမယ်။

Application ကို စ run လိုက်တာနဲ့ JPA ဟာ database မှာ table တွေ သွားဆောက်ပါတယ်။ @Entity နဲ့ ရေးထားတဲ့ class တွေ အကုန်လုံး
database table အဖြစ်နဲ့ database ထဲ အလိုအလျောက်သွားဆောက်မှာဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

ဥပမာ Entity class name က Car ဆိုရင် Car ဆိုတဲ့ table ကို အလိုလိုသွားဆောက်မှာပါ။ တကယ်လို့ ကိုယ်က Car လို့ မဆောက်ချင်ဘူး။ MotorCar လို့ ဆောက်ချင်တယ်ဆိုရင် @Entity ရဲ့ အောက်မှာ

@Table ဆိုတဲ့ annotation ကို သုံးပြီး table name ကို သီးသန့် set လုပ်လို့ရပါတယ်။

ကဲ အဲဒီတော့ example စကြည့်ကြရအောင်။ JPA နဲ့ H2 database နဲ့ သုံးဖို့ ပထမဆုံး pom.xml ဖိုင်ထဲမှာ dependencies တွေ ထပ်ထည့်ရအောင်။


<dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency> <groupId>com.h2database</groupId> <artifactId>h2</artifactId> <scope>runtime</scope>
</dependency>

Entity class ကို create လုပ်ပုံအဆင့်ဆင့် ကို ကြည့်ရအောင်။

Project Explorer မှာ root package ကို right click နှိပ်ရင် menu ပေါ်လာပါလိမ့်မယ်။
New ကို ရွေးပြီး အဲဒီထဲကမှ package ကို ရွေးပါ

package name ကို com.packt.demo.domain လို့ပေးလိုက်ပါ။

အသစ်ဆောက်ထားတဲ့ package ကို Project Explorer မှာ right click နှိပ်ပါ။ ပြီးရင် New ကိုရွေးပြီး class ကိုရွေးလိုက်ပါ။ ဒါဆိုရင် Java class အသစ်တစ်ခုရပြီ။

ပြီးရင် annotation ဖြစ်တဲ့ @Entity ကို Car class ပေါ်မှာ သတ်မှတ်ပေးလိုက်ပါ။ (@Entity ရေးပြီး Ctrl+Space ဒါမှမဟုတ် Ctrl+Shift+O ကို နှိပ်ရင် import statement အော်တိုထွက်လာပါလိမ့်မယ်။)

package com.packt.cardatabase.domain;
import javax.persistence.Entity; @Entity
public class Car { }

ပြီးရင် field တွေ(database column ဖြစ်လာမယ့်အရာတွေ) ကို define လုပ်ရအောင်။ ဒီနေရာမှာ unqiue ID ကိုလည်း ထည့်ပေးရပါလိမ့်မယ်(primary key အနေနဲ့သုံးရအောင်ပါ)။

package com.packt.demo.domain;
import javax.persistence.Entity;
import javax.persistence.GeneratedValue;
import javax.persistence.GenerationType;
import javax.persistence.Id;

@Entity
public class Car {
    @Id
    @GeneratedValue(strategy=GenerationType.AUTO)
    private long id;
    private String brand, model, color, registerNumber;
    private int yearMade, price;
}

primary key ကို define လုပ်ဖို့ @id ID ကို database ကနေ Auto generate လုပ်ပေးဖို့ @GeneratedValue

JPA provider(ဒီနေရာမှာ Hibernate) က အသင့်တော်ဆုံး ID generation ကို လုပ်ပေးပါလို့ပြောတာက (strategy=GenerationType.AUTO)

ပြီးပြီဆိုရင် field တွေအတွက် getter နဲ့ setter တွေ ထည့်ပေးရမယ်။ field တွေကို တိုက်ရိုက် set လုပ်ပေးနိုင်မယ့် constructor ထည့်ပေးရမယ်။

class ထဲမှာ cursor ကိုထားပြီး Right Click-> Source -> Generate Getter and Setters
ဆိုရင် getter setter တွေထွက်လာမယ်။

class ထဲမှာ cursor ကိုထားပြီး Right Click-> Source -> Generate Constructor using fields.... လို့ရွေး ပြီးရင် ID ကို ဖြုတ်(id က auto generate လုပ်မှာ ဆိုတော့ constructor နဲ့ assign လုပ်ရင် ထည့်မပေးရဘူး)

package com.packt.demo.domain; import javax.persistence.Entity;
import javax.persistence.GeneratedValue;
import javax.persistence.GenerationType;
import javax.persistence.Id; @Entity
public class Car { @Id @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.AUTO) private long id; private String brand, model, color, registerNumber; private int yearMade, price; public Car() { } public Car(String brand, String model, String color, String registerNumber, int yearMade, int price) { super(); this.brand = brand; this.model = model; this.color = color; this.registerNumber = registerNumber; this.yearMade = yearMade; this.price = price; } public String getBrand() { return brand; } public void setBrand(String brand) { this.brand = brand; } public String getModel() { return model; } public void setModel(String model) { this.model = model; } public String getColor() { return color; } public void setColor(String color) { this.color = color; } public String getRegisterNumber() { return registerNumber; } public void setRegisterNumber(String registerNumber) { this.registerNumber = registerNumber; } public long getId() { return id; } public void setId(long id) { this.id = id; } public int getYearMade() { return yearMade; } public void setYearMade(int yearMade) { this.yearMade = yearMade; } public int getPrice() { return price; } public void setPrice(int price) { this.price = price; }
}

ပြီးရင် H2 database နဲ့ချိတ်ဖို့ application.properties ထဲမှာ အောက်ပါအတိုင်း configure လုပ်ကြမယ်။

spring.h2.console.enabled=true
spring.datasource.platform=h2
spring.datasource.driverClassName=org.h2.Driver
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:h2:mem:test
spring.h2.console.path=/h2-console
spring.jpa.database-platform=org.hibernate.dialect.H2Dialect
spring.jpa.generate-ddl=true
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update

h2 မှာ webconsole ပါပါတယ်။ database url name က jdbc:h2:mem:test ဆိုတာကိုလည်း သတိထားမိမယ်ထင်ပါတယ်။ ကဲ Run As ->Java Application လုပ်လိုက်ပါ။ H2 database ထဲမှာ ဘာရောက်သွားလည်းကြည့်ရအောင်။
အရင်ဆုံး Spring Boot run ပြီးရင် localhost:8080/h2-console ကို browser မှာ ဖွင့်ကြည့်ပါ

url ကို jdbc:h2:mem:test လို့ပြောင်းပြီး ဝင်လိုက်ပါ။

JPA က လှမ်းပြီး create လုပ်ပေးလိုက်တဲ့ database table ဖြစ်တဲ့ Car table ကို တွေ့ရမှာဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

Items.Title: 帮助我的前端之旅的网站 | Websites that Helped My Frontend Journey
Items.Description:
老实说，每次编程之旅有时都会有点混乱。 无论是前端开发人员、后端开发人员还是全栈开发人员。 也许是因为您不知道从哪里开始，或者当您知道时，您会感到不知所措，因为要学习的语言和框架太多了。 以下是在我成为更好的前端开发人员的整个过程中帮助我的网站。

我喜欢这个网站的是路线图。 因为它不仅拥有你需要学习的所有语言和要求，而且还有一个简单的解释和参考资料，可以帮助你更多地了解该语言。 当然，它是免费的。 参考是将我们重定向到文章或教程、视频甚至免费课程的链接。

我喜欢freeCodeCamp除了它是免费的，它还带你一步一步地学习你选择的课程。 例如，假设您选择了New Responsive Web Design 课程，在课程中，您将遇到第一部分，即基本的 HTML 和 CSS。

在学习了基本的 HTML 和 CSS 之后，您可以向下滚动到下一节并开始学习更高级的 CSS，例如 Flexbox 和排版。

现在，在每个部分中，都有一个用于认证的认证项目。 完成所有认证项目后，您将收到结业证书。 您可以在您的个人资料菜单中查看您的证书。

除了真正的项目之外，我喜欢这个网站的一点是您不必设计项目。 对于免费用户，在您开始挑战后，您将获得一个 zip 文件，其中包含 .jpg 格式的设计、图像资源和风格指南。 但多亏了这一点，你还可以学习你的像素完美技能，以及侧面的细节。 如果您是专业用户，则可以访问 Sketch 和 Figma 设计文件。

前端导师也有一个Slack 社区

我喜欢这个网站的地方是它提供了很多编程语言教程，还有很多 how-tos，每个解释都经过简化以避免混淆，每个教程都有一个代码编辑器供您尝试和/或编辑代码. 总而言之，它非常适合任何级别的每个人。 该编辑器还提供浅色和深色主题。

好了，这些网站在我的前端之旅中为我提供了帮助。 在评论中让我知道哪个网站帮助了您的旅程😁。

感谢您的光临和平✌

作者



原文:

Let’s be honest, every programming journey can be a little bit confusing sometimes. Be it frontend dev, backend dev, or fullstack. Maybe it’s because you don’t know where to start or when you do know, you feel overwhelmed because there are so many languages and frameworks to learn. So here are the websites that helped me throughout my journey to be a better frontend dev.

1. roadmap.sh

roadmap.sh is a website for roadmaps, guides, and video tutorials for learning based on the role you want to be.

What I like about this web is the roadmap. Because not only it has all the language and requirements that you need to learn, but also has a simple explanation and references that can help you understand more about that language. And it’s FREE of course. The reference is a link that redirects us to an article or tutorial, video, and even free courses.

2. freeCodeCamp

freeCodeCamp is a free online course certification.

What I like about freeCodeCamp other than it’s free, it also takes you to step by step on how to learn your chosen course. For example, let’s just say you chose the New Responsive Web Design course, inside the course, you’ll encounter the first section which is the basic HTML and CSS.

And after you learned the basic HTML and CSS, you can scroll down to the next section and start the more advanced CSS such as Flexbox and typography.

Now, inside each section, there’s a certification project for your certification. After you complete all the certification projects, you will receive a certificate of completion. You can check your certificate in your profile menu.

freeCodeCamp certification is also available for Backend, Data Visualization, Quality Assurance, and many more.

3. Frontend Mentor

Frontend Mentor is a freemium website to improve you frontend skills with real projects.

What I like about this web other than its real projects is that you don’t have to design the project. For free users, after you start the challenge, you will be given a zip file containing the designs in .jpg, image assets, and the style guide. But thanks to this, you can also learn your pixel-perfect skill, and detailing on the side. If you’re a pro user, you can get access to Sketch and Figma design files.

Frontend mentor also has a Slack community

4. w3schools

W3Schools is a website for tutorials.

What I like about this website is that it offers a lot of programming language tutorials, and a lot of how-tos, every explanation is simplified to avoid confusion, and every tutorial has a code editor for you to try and/or edit the code. All in all, it’s perfect for everyone at any level. The editor is also available in light and dark theme.

W3schools also has quizzes, exercise, certification, web templates, W3Schools spaces to build your own website and discord server

And there you have it, the websites that helped me throughout my frontend journey. Let me know which website helped your journey in the comments 😁.

Thanks for coming by and Peace ✌

Items.Title: 2022 年 AWS 社区建设者假期帖子 | AWS Community Builders Holiday posts 2022
Items.Description:
热烈欢迎所有 CB。 写这个博客的动机来自@lockhead的这篇文章:)

对于 Johannes 向 CB 提出的所有问题，我又添加了一个问题 :)

是什么促使您成为 AWS 社区建设者？

当你有做某事的热情时，它会给你能量和热情去努力实现它。 我对在 AWS 上工作以及用我的经验和知识帮助技术社区充满热情，这极大地推动了我。

这就是我在 2022 年开始 AWS Community Builder 之旅的方式。我在 2021 年被授予 AWS APN 大使称号，但当我在同年离开该组织时，这个头衔也随之取消。 我记得和 Katreena Mullican 谈过我可以参与的其他项目，并继续我在技术社区的贡献。 在得知 AWS Community Builders 计划后，我立即申请，但遭到拒绝。 我在这里告诉全世界

在 2022 年结束之际，如果我回想起我作为 AWS 社区建设者的第一年旅程，我会为我所做的贡献感到无比自豪：

  • 写了4篇文章
  • 发表了 3 次演讲
  • 组织并领导了一次内部在线问答活动
  • 在全球 AWS 认证社区论坛中回答了许多问题
  • 领导巴克莱银行在印度各地的 AWS 技能提升计划，同时与 AWS T&C 团队、TAM 和 SA 密切合作
  • 作为核心组织团队成员加入 AWS User Group Pune
  • 共同组织并参与了 2022 年浦那 AWS 社区日现场活动

该计划拥有的人员、技能和资源的多样性。 另一件真正让我感到惊讶的事情是 CB 所做的贡献数量，有时我真的在想他们怎么有时间做这么多活动、博客、演讲等，以及我的贡献是否足够 :)

您的背景和使用 AWS 的经验是什么？

好吧，我正在考虑再写一篇关于它的博客 :) 但我会在这里保持简短。 我于 2007 年开始我的职业生涯，在我 16 年的职业生涯中担任过许多角色，从测试工程师、支付领域 SME、区块链架构师、AWS 解决方案架构师。 目前在巴克莱银行（印度浦那）工作，担任企业数字银行业务部门的云工程主管和首席云解决方案架构师。
我的 AWS 之旅始于 2015 年，当时我在 Linux 服务器上设置一个区块链节点，但不知道它是一个 EC2 实例：DI 从配置基础设施的人那里了解到，它是一个 EC2，是一项 AWS 服务。 从那时起，我开始探索和学习 AWS，并在 AWS 中取得了职业生涯的领先。 已将应用程序和服务器迁移到 AWS，构建了许多 AWS 解决方案，帮助客户完成了云采用之旅。 我现在拥有 6 项 AWS 认证，包括 1 项专业认证和 2 项专业认证。

您从该计划中看到的最大好处是什么？

内容和资源的可用性和访问权限、学习和提升自己技能的机会、贡献知识并与技术社区分享知识。
另一个好处是，它增加了一个人的品牌价值。 在过去的几个月里，我真的感受到了； 但它也增强了我的信心，鼓舞了我为社区做更多事情的道德。

您想获得的下一个赃物是什么？

谈论 Swags 非常重要 :) 我喜欢到目前为止从该计划收到的 swags。

你今天晚餐吃什么？ 分享食谱！

按照这里的食谱

关于 2022 年的 AWS 社区建设者计划，您还有什么要说的吗？

继续学习、协作和贡献！ 感谢@shafjag 、Taylor Lucy 和 Jason Dunn 给我这个机会和可爱的礼物！ 大家节日快乐！
圣诞快乐，新年快乐！



原文:

A very warm welcome to all the CBs. The motivation to write this blog came from this post by @lockhead :)

I'm adding one more question to all the questions Johannes asked to CBs :)

What drives you to become an AWS Community Builder ?

When you have a passion to do something, then it gives you the energy & enthusiasm to work towards achieving it. My passion to work on AWS and helping the tech community with my experience & knowledge drives me a lot.

This is how my journey to AWS Community Builder started off in 2022. I was awarded as an AWS APN Ambassador in 2021 but as I left the organization in the same year, the title was also off boarded with it. I remember talking to Katreena Mullican about other programs I can be associated with and continue my contributions in the tech community. Upon knowing about the AWS Community Builders program, I immediately applied it but got rejection. :( I then deep dived into the program objectives and started working on contributing to the wider community through blogs, answering queries in the Global AWS Certified Community forums/platforms and many other ways which eventually resulted in my application getting accepted in March 2022 :)
I told the world about it here

While wrapping the year 2022, if I recall my first year journey as an AWS Community Builder, I feel incredibly proud of the contributions I made:

  • written 4 articles
  • delivered 3 talks
  • organized & led one in-house online quiz event
  • answered many queries in the Global AWS Certified Community forum
  • Leading the AWS upskill program in Barclays, across India locations while working very closely with AWS T&C team, TAMs and SAs
  • joined the AWS User Group Pune as a core organizing team member
  • co-organized and contributed in an in-person AWS Community Day Pune 2022 event

What surprises you most about the AWS Community Builders program?

The diversity of people, skills and resources this program has. Another thing which really surprises me is the amount of contributions the CBs are making, sometimes I really think how do they get time to do so many activities, blogs, talks etc. and whether I contributed enough :)

What's your background and your experience with AWS?

Well I'm thinking on writing another blog on it :) but I'll keep it short here. I started off my career in 2007 and have played many roles in my 16 years of career ranging from Test engineer, Payments domain SME, Blockchain architect, AWS solutions architect. Currently working in Barclays (Pune, India) as a Cloud Engineering Lead and Lead Cloud Solutions Architect for the business unit called Corporate Digital Banking.
My AWS journey started in 2015 when I was setting a Blockchain node on a linux server without knowing it was an EC2 instance :D I got to know from the person who was provisioning the infra, that it was an EC2 which is an AWS service. I started exploring & learning AWS since then and took my career ahead in AWS. Have migrated applications & servers to AWS, architect many AWS solutions which helped customer in their cloud adoption journey. I now possess 6 AWS certifications including 1 professional & 2 specialty.

Whats's the biggest benefit you see from the program?

Availability and access to the contents & resources, opportunity to learn & upskill yourself, contribute and sharing knowledge with tech communities.
Another benefit is that, it increases the brand value of a person. I really felt it in last few months; but it also increases my confidence and boost my moral to do even more for the community.

What’s the next swag item that you would like to get?

Talking about Swags are incredibly important :) I loved the swags I have received till now from the program.

What are you eating for dinner today? share the recipe!

Matka Paneer Biryani (Cheese Biryani in clay pot)

Follow recipe here

Do you have anything else to say about the AWS Community Builders Program in 2022?

Keep learning, collaborating & contributing! :)
Thank you @shafjag, Taylor Lucy & Jason Dunn for this opportunity and the lovely swags! :)

Happy Holidays Everyone!
Merry Christmas and a Very Happy New Year!

Items.Title: 使用 Git 在 Cpanel 托管上使用 React + Laravel 应用程序 - 第 1 部分 | React + Laravel App on Cpanel Hosting using Git - Part 1
Items.Description:
这是我在此的头一篇博文。

我将解释如何使用 Git 在 Cpanel 共享主机上部署 React 应用程序。 确保你有 git 和 nodejs 设置。

脚手架一个新的反应应用程序

  1. 您可以使用您喜欢的包管理器，例如 yarn、pnpm 或使用 npx。

pnpm create vite@latest example-react-app

在以下位置阅读有关 vitejs 的信息： vitejs.dev/guide/

  1. 选择一个框架

  2. 选择一个变体

  3. 按照剩余的提示

启动一个git仓库

在我们新搭建的 React 应用程序中，我们将运行以下命令
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial Project"

创建一个 github 存储库

前往 github.com 并创建一个新的存储库。 您可以决定创建私有存储库。 出于本教程的目的，我将其称为cpanel-react-example

推送到github

由于我们已经启动了我们的 github 存储库，我们将推送我们现有的存储库。 现在在我们之前构建的 React 应用程序中，我们将遵循 github 命令。

完成后，我们的 github 存储库应该如下所示。

添加构建操作

我们将创建一个新文件.github/workflows/build.yml

如果您不明白这一点，在您新构建的应用程序中，您将需要创建一个文件夹.github ，然后在其中创建一个文件夹workflows 。 最后，您将在workflows文件夹中创建一个文件build.yml 。

编辑 build.yml。

 name: React App build workflow on: push: jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-20.04 strategy: matrix: node-version: [15] steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v2.2.4 with: version: 7 - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }} uses: actions/setup-node@v3 with: node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }} cache: 'pnpm' - name: Install dependencies run: pnpm install - name: Build run: pnpm run build - name: Deploy uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3 with: github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} publish_dir: ./dist

脚本所做的是构建我们的 React 应用程序，并在我们推送到main分支时将其部署到gh-pages分支。 您可以在docs.github.com/en/actions上阅读有关 github 操作的更多信息

我们将提交更改并推送到 github
git add .
git commit -m "Added github action"
git push

一旦推送，我们将有一个名为gh-pages的新分支，其中包含我们的部署

部署到主机

要部署到您的共享主机，您需要两个项目：

创建 .cpanel.yml

它包含有关如何部署 React 应用程序的说明。

导航到你的反应应用程序的公共文件夹并创建三个新文件
.cpanel.yml
.deploy.example.sh
.gitignore

我们将使用此 github 要点的内容编辑我们的文件： gist.github.com/sadiqsalau/c1b0f0e4b15e ...

任何 cpanel 部署都需要此文件。 它包含部署 React 应用程序的所有命令。 编辑它以适合您的部署。

此文件是.deploy.sh的示例。 将其.env.example文件

基于该要点中的.cpanel.yml ，当您运行第一次部署时，它将自动在您的共享主机上创建。 把它当作一个.env来导出变量，这些变量定义了你的 React 应用程序将部署到你的主机上的路径。 创建后，您应该在下一次部署之前对其进行编辑。

我们的.gitignore文件将排除.deploy.sh被 git 跟踪。

设置好所有三个文件后，就可以提交它们了。 所以我们将运行：
git add .
git commit -m "Added cpanel deploy script"
git push

生成部署密钥

打开一个新的终端并运行
ssh-keygen -t ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 -C "your_email@example.com"
注意：一些共享主机无法使用带密码的密钥克隆存储库，因此当提示输入密码时，只需按回车键。

还要注意生成的密钥的存储位置

我们将重命名我们的键，对于本教程，它们将重命名为cpanel_react_example

添加 github 部署密钥

现在转到您的 github 存储库，导航到Settings > Security > Deploy Keys并添加一个新的部署密钥。

给你的密钥一个标题，例如 Deploy key，对于密钥字段，复制你的公钥的内容（我们重命名为 cpanel_react_example.pub 的 id_ecdsa.pub）。 您可以使用文本编辑器打开公钥，或运行命令cat key_name.pub ，其中 key_name 是您的密钥名称。

作为一个例子，它应该是这样的

最后添加部署密钥。

部署到 Cpanel

登录到控制面板

导航到文件管理器

创建 .ssh 文件夹

单击设置，启用隐藏文件并创建一个名为.ssh的新文件夹（如果不存在）

上传部署密钥

打开.ssh文件夹并上传您的部署密钥

更改私钥权限

右键单击并将您的私钥的权限更改为 0600

创建 .ssh 配置

仍在我们的.ssh文件夹中创建一个名为config的新文件

编辑 .ssh 配置

编辑将example-1替换为您的 github 存储库名称，将example_1替换为您的 ssh 密钥名称。

 Host example-1.github.com HostName github.com User git IdentityFile ~/.ssh/example_1 IdentitiesOnly yes

完成后返回 cpanel 仪表板

创建一个新的 cpanel 存储库

导航到 Git 版本控制

克隆 URL 将采用这种格式
git@example-repo.github.com:example-user/example-repo.git

其他字段应自动填写

记下存储库的路径。

切换检出分支

点击管理按钮

将检出分支更改为gh-pages

运行第一个部署

切换到“ Pull or Deploy ”选项卡

在部署之前，让我们在文件管理器中打开我们的存储库以查看其内容

如果您查看列出的文件，您应该注意到没有我们之前提到的.deploy.sh 。 一旦我们运行我们的第一个部署，它就会被创建。
让我们回到存储库并部署

部署完成后，将创建一个新文件.deploy.sh 。 如果您没有部署到站点的根目录（通常是 public_html 文件夹），则应该对其进行编辑。

运行第二个部署

现在返回并再次运行部署。

部署后，导航到您在.deploy.sh中指定的路径，您应该会看到 React 应用程序。

访问您网站的网址以查看您的实时应用程序..

现在，每当您对应用程序进行更改并推送到 github 时，只需转到您的存储库，拉取然后部署。

可以在以下位置找到一个工作示例： github.com/sadiqsalau/cpanel-react-exam ...

下一部分是使用 laravel 作为 API..



原文:

This is my first post here.

I'm going to explain how to deploy a React App on a Cpanel Shared Hosting using Git. Ensure you have git and nodejs setup.

Scaffolding a new react app

  1. You can use your preferred package manager e.g yarn, pnpm or using npx.

pnpm create vite@latest example-react-app

Read about vitejs at: vitejs.dev/guide/

  1. Choose a framework

  2. Choose a variant

  3. Follow the remaining prompts

Initiate a git repository

Inside our newly scaffolded react app we are going to run the following commands
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial Project"

Creating a github repository

Head over to github.com and create a new repository. You can decide to create a private repository. For the purpose of this tutorial I will call it cpanel-react-example

Pushing to github

Since we have already initiated our github repository we are going to push our existing one. Now inside the react app we scaffolded earlier we will follow the github commands.

With that done, our github repository should look like this.

Adding the build action

We are going to create a new file .github/workflows/build.yml

If you don't understand this, inside your newly scaffolded app you will need to create a folder .github then create a folder workflows inside it. Finally you will create a file build.yml inside the workflows folder.

Edit the build.yml.

name: React App build workflow
on:
  push:
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-20.04
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [15]
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v2.2.4
      with:
        version: 7
    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        cache: 'pnpm'
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: pnpm install
    - name: Build
      run: pnpm run build
    - name: Deploy
      uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3
      with:
        github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        publish_dir: ./dist

What the script does is to build our react app and deploy it to the gh-pages branch whenever we push to the main branch. You can read more about github actions at docs.github.com/en/actions

We will commit our changes and push to github
git add .
git commit -m "Added github action"
git push

Once pushed we will have a new branch called gh-pages containing our deploy

Deploying to hosting

To deploy to your shared hosting you will need two items:

Creating .cpanel.yml

The .cpanel.yml file is like the github action script but for cpanel. It contains the instructions on how to deploy the react app.

Navigate to the public folder of your react-app and create three new files
.cpanel.yml
.deploy.example.sh
.gitignore

We will edit our files using the content of this github gist: gist.github.com/sadiqsalau/c1b0f0e4b15e…

.cpanel.yml

This file is required for any cpanel deployment. It contains all command to deploy the react app. .cpanel.yml file can be destructive so make sure you take a look at it especially the parts that make use of the command rm -rf. Edit it to suit your deployment.

.deploy.example.sh

This file is an example of .deploy.sh. Treat it like a .env.example file

.deploy.sh

Based on the .cpanel.yml in that gist, this will automatically be created on your shared hosting when you run your first deployment. Treat it like a .env that exports variables that define the path where your react app will be deployed to on your hosting. Once created you should edit it before your next deployment.

.gitignore

Our .gitignore file will exclude .deploy.sh from being tracked by git.

With all three files setup, it's time to commit them. So we will run:
git add .
git commit -m "Added cpanel deploy script"
git push

Generating deploy keys

Open a new terminal and run
ssh-keygen -t ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 -C "your_email@example.com"
Note: Some shared hosting can't clone a repository using a key with password so when prompted to enter a password just press enter.

Also take note of where the generated keys are stored

We are going to rename our keys, for this tutorial they will be renamed to cpanel_react_example

Adding github deploy keys

Now go to your github repository, navigate to Settings > Security > Deploy Keys and add a new deploy key.

Give your key a title e.g Deploy key, for the key field copy the content of your public key (id_ecdsa.pub which we renamed to cpanel_react_example.pub). You can open the public key with a text editor or run the command cat key_name.pub with key_name being the name of your key.

As an example it should look like this

Finally add the deploy key.

Deploying to Cpanel

Login to Cpanel

Navigate to file manager

Creating .ssh folder

Click settings, enable hidden files and create a new folder named .ssh if it doesn't exist

Upload deploy keys

Open .ssh folder and upload your deploy keys

Change private key permission

Right click and change the permission of your private key to 0600

Create .ssh config

Still within our .ssh folder create a new file named config

Edit .ssh config

Edit replacing example-1 with your github repository name and example_1 with your ssh key name.

Host example-1.github.com
  HostName github.com
  User git
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/example_1
  IdentitiesOnly yes

Once done return to cpanel dashboard

Creating a new cpanel repository

Navigate to Git Version Control

The Clone URL will have this format
git@example-repo.github.com:example-user/example-repo.git

The other fields should be filled automatically

Take note of the path to your repository.

Switching-checked out branch

Click on the manage button

Change the checked-out branch to gh-pages

Running first deployment

Switch to the Pull or Deploy tab

Before we deploy, let's open our repository in the file manager to see its contents

If you take a look at the files listed, you should notice that there is no .deploy.sh which we mentioned earlier. It will be created once we run our first deployment.
Let's return to the repository and deploy

Once it has been deployed a new file .deploy.sh will be created. It should be edited if you aren't deploying to the root of your site (usually the public_html folder).

Running the second deployment

Now return and run deploy once more.

Once deployed, navigate to the path where you specified in .deploy.sh, you should see the react app.

Visit the url of your site to see your live app..

Now whenever you make changes to your app and push to github just go to your repository, pull then deploy.

A working example can be found at: github.com/sadiqsalau/cpanel-react-exam…

The next part is using laravel as the API..

Items.Title: 专业设计师不惜一切代价避免网站上的这 5 个错误 | Professional designers avoid these 5 mistakes on their websites at all costs
Items.Description:
作为网页设计师，重要的是要避免犯下可能对用户体验或网站的整体有效性产生负面影响的错误。

在这篇文章中，我将提到在设计网站时要避免的 5 个非常常见的错误。

您知道您可以免费在DoTenX上使用或不使用编码来实施您的网站或登录页面吗？ 请务必检查一下，甚至提名您的作品进行展示。 让我们来看看这些常见的错误。 因为这是一个敏感的话题，所以我没有提供任何网站犯这些错误的例子，但我向您展示了避免这些错误中的每一个是如何产生巨大差异的。

对于每一点，我们都会看一些不同类别的例子。

页面拥挤

一个常见的错误是在一个页面中塞入了太多信息。 这不仅会使网站显得杂乱无章，而且会让消费者很难找到他们需要的信息。 相反，努力将文本分散在几页上，或者使用标题和副标题将其拆分，以使其更易于阅读。

例子

大型组织特别采用的技巧之一是拥有多个登录页面，帮助他们避免页面过于拥挤。

房地产 ( www.realestate.com.au/ )

这是澳大利亚最大的房地产网站之一，拥有数以万计的房产，但当您查看他们的页面时，他们会尽可能保持每个页面的简洁、简洁和切题。 这让浏览者对网站有信心，也让他们更容易尽快找到他们真正需要的东西。

使用小文本

使用太小不易阅读的文本，包括按钮或链接，是另一个需要避免的大错误。 用户，尤其是那些有视力问题或年长的用户会觉得这很烦人，并且可能无法看清小文本，无法浏览网站或获取所需的数据。 确保选择可以在所有设备和屏幕尺寸上舒适阅读的字体大小。

例子

零售 ( au.lespecs.com/ )

即使不看这家零售商的名称，您也可以轻松猜出他们在该网站上销售的商品。 根据目标受众调整字体大小非常重要。

零售 ( www.apple.com/ )

只要您避免页面过度拥挤，网页的多少部分可以使用大字体是没有限制的。

设计( www.adobe.com/ )

相反，Adobe 了解他们的目标受众，采取更激进的方法，不仅使用较小的字体，他们还使用您很可能永远不会在零售网站上看到的背景颜色。

忽略移动用户

在现代时代拥有一个适合移动设备的网站至关重要。 由于移动设备占所有互联网流量的一半以上，因此确保您的网站在移动设备上正常运行和美观至关重要。

使用不适应屏幕大小的固定宽度布局以及使用太小以至于用户无法在小屏幕上简单点击的按钮或链接是需要避免的一些典型错误。 这可能会使用户难以在移动设备上访问和参与您的网站，从而可能导致负面的用户体验。

使用响应式设计方法来确保您的网站的样式和内容适应移动设备的大小和方向，以构建适合移动设备的网站。

例子

零售 ( www.nike.com/ )

耐克不仅在移动设备上为您提供定制体验，而且还在较小的设备上提供他们的移动应用程序。

快递 ( www.dhl.com/ )

在设计良好的网站中，通常的做法是在较小的设备尺寸上隐藏某些部分、更改部分的顺序或减少每个部分的详细信息。

使用太多不同的字体

虽然在网站上使用多种字体很好，但使用太多可能会使设计显得混乱和业余。 为了统一的外观，限制您使用的字体数量，并在整个页面上一致地使用它们。

例子

社区 ( www.indiehackers.com/ )

像 Indie Hackers 这样的社区充满了文本上下文，在每个页面上几乎找不到除了小头像或表情符号之外的任何其他视觉元素。 很容易想到，使用不同的字体是为网站增添多样性的最佳方式，但实际上恰恰相反。

时尚 ( www.gucci.com/ )

重要的是要考虑您网站的上下文来决定多少种类太多。 奢侈时尚品牌 Gucci 考虑到其目标受众采用了一种更符合其设计理念的不同方法，并使用了更多的字体类型，同时保持优雅并避免过度使用。

没有使用明确的号召性用语

号召性用语 (CTA) 是网站上的一个按钮或链接，可引导访问者执行特定活动，例如查看产品页面或订阅时事通讯。 这些组件对于提高网站参与度和转化率至关重要。 它们不应模糊不清、难以查找或设计得与网站的其他部分相似。 为了吸引用户的注意力并迫使他们采取行动，他们应该引人注目并脱颖而出。

例子

市场 ( www.amazon.com/ )

您可以在任何页面上拥有多少 CTA 实际上没有限制。 一个极端的例子是亚马逊的网站。

由于亚马逊在其网站上提供的服务的性质，它应该有太多的 CTA，每个 CTA 都应该脱颖而出。 为了避免页面过度拥挤，他们为每个部分使用了极简主义设计，并且只有两种字体大小（标题和 CTA），他们帮助他们的众多 CTA 脱颖而出。

与像亚马逊这样的市场完全相反，DoTenX 采用了 SaaS 网站的典型方法。 借助 CTA，DoTenX 使用非常不同的颜色将用户引导至所需的操作。



原文:

As a web designer, it's important to avoid making mistakes that can negatively impact the user experience or the overall effectiveness of a website.

In this post, I'll mention 5 very common mistakes to avoid when designing a website.

Did you know that you can implement your websites or landing pages with or without coding on DoTenX for free? Make sure to check it out and even nominate your work to be showcased. DoTenX is open-source and you can find the repository here: github.com/dotenx/dotenx.

Let's take a look at these common mistakes. Because this is a sensitive subject I don't provide any examples for the websites making these mistakes but I show you how avoiding each of these mistakes makes a huge difference.

For each point, we'll take a look at a few examples from different categories.

Overcrowding the page

One frequent error is cramming too much information into a single page. This can not only make the website appear disorganised and overwhelming, but it can also make it challenging for consumers to discover the information they need. Instead, make an effort to spread out the text over several pages or split it up using headings and subheadings to make it simpler to read.

Examples

SaaS(www.datadoghq.com/)

DATADOG offers hundreds of services and features, but looking at their landing page you see very specific and well-separated sections without overcrowding the page.

One of the tricks that larger organisations particularly do is to have multiple landing pages helping them to avoid overcrowded pages.

Real estate (www.realestate.com.au/)

This is one of the largest real-estate websites in Australia with tens of thousands of properties, yet when you look at their pages they keep each of them as clean, minimal and to the point as possible. This gives the viewers a sense of confidence in the website, and also makes it a lot easier for them to find what they actually need as quickly as possible.

Using tiny text

Using texts, including on buttons or links, which are too small to read easily is another huge mistake to avoid. Users, especially those who have visual problems or are elderly can find this annoying and may have trouble seeing small texts and not be able to navigate around the website or get the data they need. Make sure to choose a font size that can be read comfortably on all devices and screen sizes.

Examples

Retail (au.lespecs.com/)

Even without reading the name of this retailer, you can easily guess what they sell on this website. It's really important to adjust the font size based on your target audience.

Retail (www.apple.com/)

Apple's website is another example of how important it is to use fonts large enough to easily read the information the company wants to convey. There is no limit on how many sections of a web page can use the larges font size as long as you avoid overcrowding the page.

Design(www.adobe.com/)

On the contrary, Adobe, knowing their target audience, take a more radical approach, and not only use smaller fonts, they use background colors that you most likely will never see on a retail website.

Ignoring mobile users

It is essential to have a mobile-friendly website in the modern era. Since mobile devices account for more than half of all internet traffic, it's critical to ensure that your website functions and looks great on them.

Use of fixed-width layouts that don't adapt to the size of the screen and the use of buttons or links that are too small for users to simply tap on a small screen are some typical mistakes to avoid. This may make it challenging for users to access and engage with your website on mobile devices, which may result in a negative user experience.

Use responsive design approaches to guarantee that your website's style and content adjust to the size and orientation of mobile devices in order to build a mobile-friendly website.

Examples

Retail (www.nike.com/)

Nike not only gives you a custom experience on mobile devices, but they also offer their mobile app on smaller devices.

Courier (www.dhl.com/)

It's a common practice in well-designed websites to hide some sections on smaller device sizes, change the order of sections or reduce the details of each section.

Using too many different fonts

Although using multiple fonts on a website is fine, utilising too many might make the design appear cluttered and amateurish. For a unified appearance, limit the number of typefaces you employ and utilise them consistently across the entire page.

Examples

Community (www.indiehackers.com/)

A community such as Indie Hackers is full of textual context and barely any other visual elements other than small profile pictures or emojis can be found on each page. It's easy to think, using different fonts is the best way to add variety to the website but in reality, the opposite is true.

Fashion (www.gucci.com/)

It's important to consider the context of your website to decide how much variety is too much. The luxury fashion house Gucci, considering its target audience takes a different approach that better matches its design philosophy and uses a larger set of font types while maintaining elegance and avoiding overdoing it.

Not using clear calls to action

A call to action (CTA) is a button or link on a website that directs visitors to perform a particular activity, such as viewing a product page or subscribing to a newsletter. These components are essential for increasing website engagement and conversion. CTAs need to be clearly visible and simple to find in order to be effective.

They shouldn't be obscured, difficult to find, or designed to look like the rest of the website. In order to attract the user's attention and compel them to act, they should instead be conspicuously noticeable and stand out.

Examples

Marketplace (www.amazon.com/)

There is virtually no limit on how many CTAs you can have on any page. An extreme example of this is Amazon's website.

Due to the nature of the service Amazon offers on its website it should have too many CTAs each of which should stand out. To avoid overcrowding the page, they have used a minimalist design for each section and with only two font sizes (title and CTA) they have helped their numerous CTAs to stand out.

DoTenX (https://dotenx.com)

Exactly opposite to a marketplace like Amazon, DoTenX takes an approach that is typical of SaaS websites. With CTAs, DoTenX uses very different colours to direct the users towards the desired actions.

雷切尔·帕默

这是一个巨大的人数，我想看看炒作是否真实。 我决定尝试使用一种我不知道的编程语言（Golang）构建一些应用程序，看看它能有多成功。

我的基本规则

  • 不要阅读很多 Go 文档。 如果我是编写大部分代码或花时间阅读文档的人，那将是失败的
  • 不要尝试任何太花哨的东西……但也不要在 Copilot 上手下留情。 这不是营销粉扑片。 我真的很想知道什么是可能的。

实验一：Hello World

安装 go、VS Code go 扩展和 Github Copilot 扩展后，我创建了一个新文件，浏览了一个教程，然后输入
package main
import
判决：见鬼，这行得通！

实验 2：构建自己的容器

对于更困难的事情，我转向了这个构建你自己的容器教程。 我输入
package main
import(
副驾驶建议fmt 。
接受这个建议后，我输入了os ，Copilot 建议输入os/exec 。
然后，我输入func ... 并且 Copilot 偏离了航线：
main() { binary, lookErr := exec.LookPath("ls") if lookErr != nil { panic(lookErr) } args := []string{"ls", "-a", "-l", "-h"} env := os.Environ() execErr := syscall.Exec(binary, args, env) if execErr != nil { panic(execErr)

判决：没有。

我输入switch os.Args[1]{ ，然后 Copilot 帮助提供：

case "run": parent() case "child": child() default: panic("what?") }

我们重回正轨！

我继续学习本教程，结论是虽然 Copilot 有时会为我想要的东西提供错误的建议，但代码本身很棒。 如果我只给它一点点命中，它就能给我整个代码块，准确率超过 90%。

最终代码几乎与教程完全匹配。

判决：...

最终，我对可能发生的事情印象深刻。 我仍然不了解 golang，也不太可能学习，但我确实觉得我能够在几周内使用 Gopilot 之类的东西作为一种“超级 linter”来构建应用程序。

原文:

rachelle palmer

Posted on Dec 24

It's been just a few months since Github made Copilot, and yet the new Github Copilot extension for VS Code already has more than 2 million downloads. This is a huge amount of people, and I wanted to see if the hype is real. I decided to try and build a few apps with a programming language that I don't know - Golang - and see how successful it might be.

My Ground Rules

  • Don't read a lot of Go docs. If I'm the one writing most of the code, or spending time in the docs, that would be a fail
  • Don't try anything TOO fancy... but don't go easy on Copilot either. This is not a marketing puff piece. I really want to know what's possible.

Experiment 1: Hello World

After installing go, the VS Code go extension, and the Github Copilot extension, I created a new file, skimmed one tutorial, and typed
package main
import
Github Copilot then suggested fmt
Github then suggested func main() once I accepted this suggestion, GC replied with

{fmt.PrintIn("Hello World!")
}


Verdict: Hell yea, this works!

Experiment 2: Build Your Own Container

For something a little more difficult, I turned to this Build Your Own Container tutorial. I typed
package main
import(
and Copilot suggested fmt.
After accepting this suggestion, I typed os, and Copilot suggested os/exec.
Then, I typed func... and Copilot veered off course with this:
main() { binary, lookErr := exec.LookPath("ls") if lookErr != nil { panic(lookErr) } args := []string{"ls", "-a", "-l", "-h"} env := os.Environ() execErr := syscall.Exec(binary, args, env) if execErr != nil { panic(execErr)

}

Verdict: No.

I typed switch os.Args[1]{ and from there Copilot helpfully offers:

case "run": parent() case "child": child() default: panic("what?") }

And we're back on track!

I continue on with this tutorial and the verdict is that although Copilot sometimes offers wrong suggestions for what I want, the code itself is great. If I give it just a tiny hit, it can give me entire blocks of code that are more than 90% accurate.

Final code matches the tutorial almost exactly.

Verdict:...

Ultimately I was pretty impressed by what is possible. I still don't know golang and I'm unlikely to learn, but I did feel like I'd be capable of building an application within a matter of weeks with something like Gopilot as a sort of 'uber linter`.

原文:

Gabriel J

Posted on Dec 24

DevOps é um termo ligado a área de desenvolvimento de sistemas e infraestruturas na área de TI. É um termo que conecta as palavras Development e Operations (Desenvolvimento e Operações), está fortemente ligada a automatização de processos de desenvolvimento e infraestrutura.

Definir DevOps é algo simplesmente complexo de se fazer, pois é considerada prática, conjunto de ferramentas e até mesmo uma filosofia de trabalho, além de cultura de como trabalhar.

As principais praticas adotadas por DevOps são:

CI - Continuous Integration -> Integração continua

CD - Continuous Delivery -> Entrega Continua

IAC - Infrastructure as Code -> Infraestrutura como codigo

Logging and Nonitoring -> Monitoramento e registro de logs

Communication and colaboration -> Comunicação e colaboração

Microservices -> Microserviços.

O uso dessas praticas trazem 5 beneficios principais ao DevOps:

1- Velocidade de desenvolvimento e entrega rápida e continua.

2- Confiabilidade.

3- Escalabilidade.

4- Melhora na colaboração das equipes.

5- Segurança nas operações

Algumas das ferramentas utilizadas para DevOps estão:

Ferramentas de controle de versão.
Ex: GitHub, Subversion, GitBucket.

Ferramentas de Orquestração de DevOps
Ex: Cerberon

Gerenciamento de configuração.
Ex: Puppet

Integração continua.
Ex: Jenkins, Go, Teamcity

Depuração.
Ex: Fabric, Capistrano

Monitoramento.
Ex: Splunk, Nagios, Loggly

Controle de logs.
Ex: Flume, ELK, Fluentd

ซีรี่ย์เกาหลี Reborn Rich ซับไทย ฮยอนอูทำงานให้กับซุนยังกรุ๊ปมานานกว่า 10 ปี เขาบริการตระกูลเจ้าของกิจการอย่างซื่อสัตย์เหมือนคนรับใช้ อยู่มาวันหนึ่งเขาถูกรองประธานหักหลังและถูกฆาตกรรม กระทั่งเขาได้กลับชาติมาเกิดในร่างของโดจุน หลานชายคนสุดท้องของจินยังชอล ผู้ก่อตั้งซุนยังกรุ๊ป ตอนนี้เขาวางแผนที่จะแก้แค้นตระกูลที่เขาเคยรับใช้และครอบครองบริษัท

Reborn Rich ตอนที่ 16 ซับไทย Created: 24 ธ.ค. 2022 Lee Sung MinReborn Rich Reborn Rich (2022) ซับไทยReborn Rich ตอนที่ 16 ซับไทยShin Hyun BeenSong Joong Kiชินฮยอนบินซงจุงกิซีรี่ย์เกาหลี Reborn Rich ซับไทยดูย้อนหลัง Reborn Rich (2022) ซับไทยตอนจบ Reborn Rich (2022) ซับไทยตอนที่15 Reborn Rich (2022) ซับไทยตอนล่าสุด Reborn Rich (2022) ซับไทยตอนแรก Reborn Rich (2022) ซับไทยตอนใหม่ Reborn Rich (2022) ซับไทยอีซองมินเว็บดูซีรี่ย์

สำหรับแฟน ๆ ชาวไทยก็เตรียมรอชมกันได้เลย เพราะว่า Reborn Rich จะได้ลงจอให้ชมพร้อม ๆ กับฝั่งเกาหลี โดยลิขสิทธิ์ได้อยู่ในมือของสตรีมมิ่งเจ้าดัง อย่าง VIU เรียบร้อยแล้ว เริ่มตอนแรกในวันที่ 18 พฤศจิกายนนี้

เรื่องย่อซีรีส์ Reborn Rich
ซีรีส์ที่อ้างอิงมาจากนิยายออนไลน์ในชื่อเดียวกัน ซึ่งจะเล่าเรื่องราวเกี่ยวกับชายหนุ่มที่ถูกฆาตกรรม หลังจากที่เขาถูกใส่ความว่าทำการยักยอกโดยตระกูลมหาเศรษฐีที่เขาจงรักภักดีมามากกว่า 10 ปี เขาได้กลับมามีชีวิตใหม่อีกครั้งในฐานะลูกชายคนเล็กสุดของตระกูลแชบอล (ตระกูลเศรษฐี) โดยเขาได้วางแผนล้างแค้น ด้วยการค่อย ๆ เข้าไปยึดกิจการของตระกูล

นักแสดงซีรีส์ Reborn Rich ซงจุงกิ รับบทเป็น ยุนฮยอนอู & จินโดจุน

ชายหนุ่มที่ตายหลังจากถูกจับเข้าคุกด้วยการถูกใส่ความว่าทำการยักยอกเงินจาก ซุนยางกรุ๊ป ธุรกิจของครอบครัวหนึ่งซึ่งเขาจงรักภักดีอย่างมาก เขาได้มาเกิดใหม่ในฐานะลูกชายคนเล็กสุดของตระกูลแชบอล (ตระกูลเศรษฐี) จินโดจุน โดยเขาได้ค่อย ๆ แฝงตัวเข้าไปยึดกิจการของตระกูลเพื่อล้างแค้น

อีซองมิน รับบทเป็น จินยางชอล
หัวหน้าผู้ดูแล ซุนยางกรุ๊ป ผู้ยึดติดอยู่กับการแข่งขันอย่างไม่ลดละและมีบุคลิกที่รุนแรง พร้อมกับความโลภในทรัพย์สินเงินทองอย่างไม่มีที่สิ้นสุด

ชินฮยอนบิน รับบทเป็น ซอมินยอง
อัยการแผนกสืบสวนต่อต้านทุจริตคอรัปชั่นที่ทำงานอยู่ที่สำนักอัยการกลางกรุงโซล เธอเป็นคนที่มีพื้นฐานครอบครัวที่ร่ำรวยและมาจากตระกูลสายกฎหมาย อย่างไรก็ตามในการทำงาน ซอมินยอง เต็มใจจะหลบหลีกหรือฝ่าฝืนกฎหมายเพื่อความยุติธรรมที่ควรจะเป็น ซึ่ง ซอมินยอง และ จินโดจุน ได้มีความสัมพันธ์ที่อยู่บนเส้นระหว่าง ความรัก และ ความเกลียดชัง ต่อกัน

Items.Title: 在嵌入式系统中处理中断的技巧 | Tips for working with interrupts in embedded systems
Items.Description:
中断是嵌入式系统的一个共同特征，允许处理器暂时停止其当前任务并执行特定例程以响应外部事件。 中断可以由多种来源触发，例如定时器、按钮按下或传感器读数。 使用中断可能具有挑战性，因为如果实施不当，它们会影响系统的性能和可靠性。 以下是在嵌入式系统中处理中断的一些技巧：

谨慎使用中断：中断会显着影响系统的性能，因为它们会导致处理器不断地在任务之间切换。 因此，重要的是仅在必要时使用中断并将其频率降至最低。 确定中断的优先级：某些中断可能比其他中断更重要，因此相应地确定它们的优先级很重要。 例如，与安全相关的关键中断应优先于非关键任务。 使用高效的中断处理程序：中断处理程序应设计为快速执行并最大限度地减少它们执行的处理量。 如果一个中断处理程序需要很长时间才能执行，它会延迟其他中断的执行并影响系统的整体性能。 避免在中断处理程序中使用阻塞函数：阻塞函数（例如 delay() 或 while 循环）应避免在中断处理程序中使用，因为它们会阻止处理器响应其他中断。 彻底测试和调试中断：彻底测试和调试中断以确保它们按预期工作并且不会引起任何问题非常重要。 这可能涉及使用调试器或其他测试工具来单步执行代码并找出任何问题。

总的来说，在嵌入式系统中处理中断需要仔细规划并注意细节。 通过遵循这些技巧和最佳实践，开发人员可以确保他们的中断得到有效实施，并且不会对系统的性能和可靠性产生负面影响。

原文:

Interrupts are a common feature of embedded systems, allowing the processor to temporarily halt its current task and execute a specific routine in response to an external event. Interrupts can be triggered by a variety of sources, such as a timer, a button press, or a sensor reading. Working with interrupts can be challenging, as they can impact the performance and reliability of the system if not implemented correctly. Here are some tips for working with interrupts in embedded systems:

Use interrupts sparingly: Interrupts can significantly impact the performance of the system, as they cause the processor to constantly switch between tasks. Therefore, it is important to use interrupts only when necessary and to minimize their frequency. Prioritize interrupts: Some interrupts may be more important than others, and it is important to prioritize them accordingly. For example, a critical safety-related interrupt should take precedence over a non-critical task. Use efficient interrupt handlers: Interrupt handlers should be designed to execute quickly and minimize the amount of processing they perform. If an interrupt handler takes a long time to execute, it can delay the execution of other interrupts and impact the overall performance of the system. Avoid using blocking functions in interrupt handlers: Functions that block, such as delay() or while loops, should be avoided in interrupt handlers as they can prevent the processor from responding to other interrupts. Test and debug interrupts thoroughly: It is important to thoroughly test and debug interrupts to ensure that they are working as intended and not causing any issues. This may involve using a debugger or other testing tools to step through the code and identify any problems.

Overall, working with interrupts in embedded systems requires careful planning and attention to detail. By following these tips and best practices, developers can ensure that their interrupts are implemented efficiently and do not negatively impact the performance and reliability of the system.

Items.Title: 在嵌入式系统中针对大小优化代码的技巧 | Tips for optimizing code for size in embedded systems
Items.Description:
在嵌入式系统的上下文中，优化代码的大小通常很重要，因为大多数嵌入式系统上的可用内存是有限的。 针对大小优化代码涉及最小化代码占用的内存量，这有助于确保软件符合目标硬件平台的限制，并且不会导致性能问题。 以下是在嵌入式系统中针对大小优化代码的一些技巧：

使用较小的数据类型：优化代码大小的一种方法是尽可能使用较小的数据类型。 例如，使用 byte 而不是 int 可以节省四个字节的内存。 避免不必要的变量：声明不必要的变量会增加代码的大小，因此只声明实际需要的变量很重要。 明智地使用数组和结构：数组和结构对于组织数据很有用，但它们也会增加代码的大小。 明智地且仅在必要时使用它们很重要。 最小化函数调用：函数调用会增加代码的开销，因此尽可能减少函数调用的次数非常重要。 这可能涉及内联函数或使用宏而不是函数。 使用优化标志：大多数编译器都提供优化标志，可以帮助减少代码的大小。 这些标志可能包括选项，例如删除未使用的代码或最小化数据结构的大小。

总的来说，在嵌入式系统中针对大小优化代码需要仔细规划并注意细节。 通过遵循这些提示和最佳实践，开发人员可以确保他们的代码尽可能小和高效，这有助于提高系统的性能和可靠性。

原文:

In the context of embedded systems, it is often important to optimize code for size, as the available memory on most embedded systems is limited. Optimizing code for size involves minimizing the amount of memory that the code occupies, which can help ensure that the software fits within the constraints of the target hardware platform and does not cause performance issues. Here are some tips for optimizing code for size in embedded systems:

Use smaller data types: One way to optimize code for size is to use smaller data types where possible. For example, using a byte instead of an int can save four bytes of memory. Avoid unnecessary variables: Declaring unnecessary variables can increase the size of the code, so it is important to only declare variables that are actually needed. Use arrays and structs judiciously: Arrays and structs can be useful for organizing data, but they can also increase the size of the code. It is important to use them wisely and only when necessary. Minimize function calls: Function calls can add overhead to the code, so it is important to minimize the number of function calls where possible. This may involve inlining functions or using macros instead of functions. Use optimization flags: Most compilers offer optimization flags that can help reduce the size of the code. These flags may include options such as removing unused code or minimizing the size of data structures.

Overall, optimizing code for size in embedded systems requires careful planning and attention to detail. By following these tips and best practices, developers can ensure that their code is as small and efficient as possible, which can help improve the performance and reliability of the system.

Items.Title: TDD 实践：成功的技巧和最佳实践 | TDD in Practice: Tips and Best Practices for Success
Items.Description:
测试驱动开发 (TDD) 是一种软件开发方法，涉及在编写代码本身之前为代码编写测试。 乍一看这似乎违反直觉，但 TDD 具有许多优势，使其成为任何软件开发过程的重要组成部分。

以下是在软件开发项目中实践 TDD 的一些技巧和最佳实践：

编写小而集中的测试

在为您的代码编写测试时，保持它们小而集中是很重要的。 每个测试都应该测试代码的特定方面，并且它应该只包含使测试通过所需的最少代码量。 这有助于您确保测试彻底和完整，还可以更轻松地调试可能出现的任何问题。

写代码前先写测试

这有助于您在编写代码之前确定代码的具体要求和期望，从而帮助您设计和构建更好的软件。 它还可以帮助您在开发过程的早期发现并修复代码的任何问题，从长远来看可以节省您的时间和精力。

测试正面和负面场景

编写测试时，测试正面和负面场景很重要。 这意味着测试您的代码预计会正常工作的情况，以及预计会失败的情况。 这有助于您识别代码的任何潜在问题，并确保其健壮可靠。

将测试驱动开发与其他测试方法结合使用

它最好与其他测试方法结合使用，例如集成测试、端到端测试和持续集成。 通过结合使用这些方法，您可以确保您的软件经过全面测试并具有高质量。

使测试成为您开发过程的一部分

测试应该是软件开发过程中不可或缺的一部分，TDD 可以通过确保测试是强制性步骤来提供帮助。 确保为测试分配足够的时间和资源，并确保您的开发团队接受过 TDD 最佳实践方面的培训。 通过将测试放在首位，您可以确保您的软件具有高质量并满足用户的需求。

总之，TDD 是一个有价值的工具，可以帮助您设计、构建和维护高质量的软件。 通过遵循这些技巧和最佳实践，您可以在软件开发项目中有效地实施 TDD，并从这种强大的方法中获益。



原文:

Test-Driven Development (TDD) is a software development methodology that involves writing tests for your code before writing the code itself. This might seem counterintuitive at first, but TDD has a number of benefits that make it an essential part of any software development process.

Here are a few tips and best practices for practicing TDD in your software development projects:

Write small, focused tests

When writing tests for your code, it's important to keep them small and focused. Each test should test a specific aspect of your code, and it should only include the minimum amount of code necessary to make the test pass. This helps you ensure that your tests are thorough and complete, and it also makes it easier to debug any issues that may arise.

Write tests before writing code

One of the key principles of TDD is to write tests before writing code. This helps you identify the specific requirements and expectations of your code before you write it, which can help you design and build better software. It also helps you catch and fix any issues with your code early in the development process, which can save you time and effort in the long run.

Test for both positive and negative scenarios

When writing tests, it's important to test for both positive and negative scenarios. This means testing for cases where your code is expected to work correctly, as well as cases where it is expected to fail. This helps you identify any potential issues with your code and ensures that it is robust and reliable.

Use test-driven development in combination with other testing methods

TDD is just one part of a comprehensive testing strategy. It is best used in combination with other testing methods, such as integration testing, end-to-end testing, and continuous integration. By using a combination of these methods, you can ensure that your software is thoroughly tested and of high quality.

Make testing a part of your development process

Testing should be an integral part of your software development process and TDD can help by ensuring that tests are a mandatory step. Make sure to allocate sufficient time and resources for testing, and make sure that your development team is trained in TDD best practices. By making testing a priority, you can ensure that your software is of high quality and meets the needs of your users.

In conclusion, TDD is a valuable tool that can help you design, build, and maintain high-quality software. By following these tips and best practices, you can effectively implement TDD in your software development projects and reap the benefits of this powerful methodology.

Items.Description:

但是有一些场景我们需要将它推迟到运行时。 让我展示书中示例之一的简化​​版本

我们有一个方法的特点。 此方法内部不允许更改内部状态。 如果我们想存储发送的消息怎么办？ 由于某些原因（例如来自第三方），我们无法修改此特征，因此我们无法更改为&mut self 。 无论如何，让我们先做，看看会发生什么。

 struct MyMessenger { messages: Vec<String> } impl MyMessenger { fn new() -> MyMessenger { MyMessenger { messages: vec![] } } } impl Messenger for MyMessenger { fn send(&self, msg: &str) { self.messages.push(String::from(msg)); } }

在调用push方法时，编译器会报错。

 cannot borrow `self.messages` as mutable, as it is behind a `&` reference `self` is a `&` reference, so the data it refers to cannot be borrowed as mutablerustcClick for full compiler diagnostic main.rs(2, 13): consider changing that to be a mutable reference: `&mut self`

 use std::cell::RefCell; struct MyMessenger { messages: RefCell<Vec<String>> } impl MyMessenger { fn new() -> MyMessenger { MyMessenger { messages: RefCell::new(vec![]) } } } impl Messenger for MyMessenger { fn send(&self, msg: &str) { self.messages.borrow_mut().push(String::from(msg)); } }

我们使用borrow_mut来借用一个可变值。 这次编译通过。

原文:

Franz Wong

Posted on Dec 24

Rust does compile time check on borrowing rule. But there are some scenarios we need to defer it to runtime. Let me show a simplified version from one of the examples in the book

We have a trait with one method. No change on internal states is allowed inside this method. (&self is used instead of &mut self)

pub trait Messenger { fn send(&self, msg: &str);
}

What if we want to store the messages sent? (e.g. it's common when we create a mock object for testing) It means we need to change the internal states. For some reasons (e.g. it is from 3rd party), we can't modify this trait, so we can't change to &mut self. Anyway, let's do it first and see what will happen.

struct MyMessenger { messages: Vec<String>
} impl MyMessenger { fn new() -> MyMessenger { MyMessenger { messages: vec![] } }
} impl Messenger for MyMessenger { fn send(&self, msg: &str) { self.messages.push(String::from(msg)); }
}

On the line push method is called, the compiler complains.

cannot borrow `self.messages` as mutable, as it is behind a `&` reference

`self` is a `&` reference, so the data it refers to cannot be borrowed as mutablerustcClick for full compiler diagnostic

main.rs(2, 13): consider changing that to be a mutable reference: `&mut self`

We want to make it passes the compilation. Let's rewrite it with RefCell.

use std::cell::RefCell; struct MyMessenger { messages: RefCell<Vec<String>>
} impl MyMessenger { fn new() -> MyMessenger { MyMessenger { messages: RefCell::new(vec![]) } }
} impl Messenger for MyMessenger { fn send(&self, msg: &str) { self.messages.borrow_mut().push(String::from(msg)); }
}

We use borrow_mut to borrow a mutable value. Compilation passes this time.

The borrowing rule is still checked in runtime. Runtime error will occur if we do this.

(This violates the rule "At any given time, we can have either one mutable reference or any number of immutable references".)

let x = RefCell::new(String::from("Hello World"));
let y = x.borrow_mut();
let z = x.borrow(); // Error occurs here in runtime

Jaime Morgan Hitchcock - Sep 3

Donald Sebastian Leung - Aug 31

Items.Title: 消除 CLOS 以获得 4.5 倍的加速 | Eliminating CLOS for a 4.5x speedup
Items.Description:
我的朋友 Nathan 今天联系我，请我帮助优化他正在构建的一些奇怪语言的词法分析器。 嗯，实际上，我们的谈话开始了，

内森·林戈

唉，真希望 CL 有 &mut 引用，而不是使用宏而不是函数

菲比戈德曼

我不希望那样
但它们很容易使用 clojures 或 conses 来模拟，比如 Cell

内森·林戈

我的意思是我不希望它们像生锈一样普遍
但是在我的解析器中，我有一堆状态，我想分配堆栈，但在事物之间共享

我头脑风暴了一些他可以实现的方法（我提出的选项是将状态放在一个结构和dynamic-extent分配一个实例，或者将它放在一个let中并将所有操作它的函数写在一个内部labels中）。 但最终他无意中透露，他实际上想让他的词法分析器以 1MiB/s 或更快的速度运行，并且堆栈分配其状态是他如何做到这一点的想法之一。

现在，不要听起来太自负，但我认为自己是优化 Common Lisp 代码的专家。 我告诉他要做的第一件事是使用(optimize (speed 3) (safety 1) (debug 0))进行编译。 奇怪的是，效果非常依赖于平台。 在 Intel i7 上，加速了几个百分点，但在 Arm A72 上，它慢了几个百分点，因为不知何故它收集了更多的垃圾。 请记住，我们并不是特别收集严格的数据； 我们一直在寻找唾手可得的成果，而不是微观优化。

我让他把源发给我，结果发现他所有的数据都在 CLOS 类中。 但它慢得要命。 一旦你开始弄乱 MOP，它就会变得更慢。 我提倡的策略是，使用 CLOS 开发您的原型，然后，如果您需要它更快，将其转换为使用defstruct 。 所以这就是我所做的。

方便的是， defstruct已经支持用只读槽定义结构，所以我们甚至不必求助于我的自由放任“只是不要改变它”的态度。 我在他的项目中 grep 了defclass ，用类似的defstruct替换了每个实例，然后用结构构造函数替换了所有的make-instance调用，以达到很好的效果。 他没有使用多重继承（或者实际上根本没有使用任何继承），因此更改是微不足道的，而且纯粹是语法上的。 无需更改逻辑。

所以我在我的 m1 MacBook Air 上运行了他的基准测试，而且加速是荒谬的。 对于旧版本，SBCL的time报道：

 Evaluation took: 3.155 seconds of real time 3.112716 seconds of total run time (2.781656 user, 0.331060 system) [ Run times consist of 0.358 seconds GC time, and 2.755 seconds non-GC time. ] 98.67% CPU 66 lambdas converted 1,614,459,488 bytes consed

新版本只是用defstruct结构替换了 CLOS 类，显示：

 Evaluation took: 0.680 seconds of real time 0.664909 seconds of total run time (0.543139 user, 0.121770 system) [ Run times consist of 0.125 seconds GC time, and 0.540 seconds non-GC time. ] 97.79% CPU 15 lambdas converted 405,298,304 bytes consed

很明显，3.155s 是 0.680s 的 4.5 倍。 而且，CLOS 版本花费大约 4 倍来生成相同数量的对象（或者至少是他的代码可见的对象）。 这并没有让我感到特别惊讶，但我认为对于不太熟悉 Common Lisp 内部结构的人来说可能会出乎意料。 简而言之，CLOS 是市场上最强大的对象系统，要有效地实施它真的很难。 特别是，支持类和方法的动态（重新）定义意味着有很多状态、很多查找和很多缓存。

我想指出，以防万一：这里的教训不是“不要使用 CLOS，因为它很慢”。 教训是，使用 CLOS 编写代码，如果您担心需要它的速度快，请坚持使用与defstruct对应的 CLOS 子集。 然后，一旦你到达真正关心性能的地方，尝试用结构替换你的类。 从您构造了很多实例并经常访问的类开始； 很少使用的课程无关紧要。

现在就去给你的朋友留下深刻印象吧，你有能力在 10 分钟的编辑后让他们的代码运行速度快好几倍！



原文:

My friend Nathan pinged me today asking me for help optimizing the lexer for some weird language he's building. Well, actually, our conversation started,

Nathan Ringo

Sigh, rly wish CL had &mut references other than using macros instead of functions

phoebe Goldman

i do not wish that
but they’re easy to emulate using clojures or conses as something like Cell

Nathan Ringo

I mean I don't wish they were as pervasive as in rust
But in eg my parser I've got a bunch of state I'd like to be stack allocated, but shared between things

I brainstormed some ways he could accomplish that (the options I proposed were either putting the state in a struct and dynamic-extent allocating an instance, or putting it in a let and writing all the functions that operate on it in an inner labels). But eventually he let slip that he actually wanted to get his lexer to run at 1MiB/s or faster, and stack-allocating its state was one of his thoughts of how he might do it.

Now, not to sound too full of myself, but I consider myself an expert in optimizing Common Lisp code. The first thing I told him to do was to compile with (optimize (speed 3) (safety 1) (debug 0)). Oddly, the effects were very platform-dependent; on an Intel i7, that saw a speedup of a few percent, but on an Arm A72, it was a few percent slower, because somehow it garbage collected more. Keep in mind that we weren't particularly collecting rigorous data; we were looking for low-hanging fruit, not micro-optimizations.

I asked him to send me the source, and it turned out that all of his data was in CLOS classes. (More specifically, he'd worked out some whacky arcana with the metaobject protocol to make CLOS classes with read-only slots. I've always said "just don't mutate them," but hey, to each their own, I guess.) Now I love CLOS. But it's slow as hell. And it gets way slower as soon as you start messing with the MOP. The strategy I advocate for is, develop your prototype using CLOS, and then, if you need it to be faster, convert it to using defstruct. So that's what I did.

Conveniently, defstruct already supports defining structures with read-only slots, so we didn't even have to resort to my laissez-faire "just don't mutate it" attitude. I grepped for defclass in his project, replaced every instance with a similar defstruct, and then replaced all the make-instance calls with structure constructors for good measure. He wasn't using multiple inheritance (or any inheritance at all, actually), so the changes were trivial, and purely syntactic. No changes to logic required.

So I ran his benchmark on my m1 MacBook Air, and the speedup was absurd. For the old version, SBCL's time reported:

Evaluation took:
  3.155 seconds of real time
  3.112716 seconds of total run time (2.781656 user, 0.331060 system)
  [ Run times consist of 0.358 seconds GC time, and 2.755 seconds non-GC time. ]
  98.67% CPU
  66 lambdas converted
  1,614,459,488 bytes consed

The new version, just replacing CLOS classes with defstruct structs, showed:

Evaluation took:
  0.680 seconds of real time
  0.664909 seconds of total run time (0.543139 user, 0.121770 system)
  [ Run times consist of 0.125 seconds GC time, and 0.540 seconds non-GC time. ]
  97.79% CPU
  15 lambdas converted
  405,298,304 bytes consed

So obviously, 3.155s is like 4.5x as long as 0.680s. But also, the CLOS version is consing roughly 4x as much to produce the same number of objects (or at least, objects visible to his code). This doesn't particularly surprise me, but I think it may be unexpected to people less familiar with the internals of Common Lisp. The short version is that CLOS is the most powerful object system on the market, and implementing it efficiently is really hard. In particular, supporting dynamic (re-)definition of classes and methods means there's a lot of state, a lot of lookups, and a lot of caching.

I'd like to point out, just in case: the lesson here is not "don't use CLOS because it's slow." CLOS is a fantastic tool, especially for interactive development. The lesson is, write your code with CLOS, and if you're concerned about needing it to be fast, stick to the subset of CLOS that corresponds to defstruct. Then, once you get to a place of actually caring about performance, try replacing your classes with structs. Start with the classes that you construct many instances of and access a lot; rarely-used classes won't matter.

Now go forth and impress your friends with your ability to make their code run several times faster in 10 minutes of editing!

Items.Title: 矿物质不足 | Not Enough Minerals
Items.Description:
1. 流水线感觉就像汇编代码
  2. 构建每个蓝图
  3. 我可以使用递归吗？
  4. 我的递归函数的参数
  5. 让条件暴露我的障碍
  6. 没有足够的计算机科学技能

我对这个谜题感觉不太好：

  • 每个蓝图都需要玩几个场景
  • 意思是，所有这一切的基础是一个图形数据结构，其中填充了每个蓝图在随后的每一分钟内可用的每个可能的移动
  • 我不擅长图表
  • 我仍然被递归和搜索算法吓倒

对我有利的工作：

  • 我明白这个问题
  • 我知道我可以将蓝图列表解析为每个机器人的矿物成本字典
  • 我觉得我可以玩一个场景，当一个能够制造下一种矿物的机器人可以被制造出来时，我的代码就会制造它

问题出在分支机构，工厂可以选择等待更多资源来制造更多类型较小的机器人，而不是更接近晶洞机器人的机器人。

让我们看看这是怎么回事，我能走多远！

构建每个蓝图

  • 示例输入具有方便的换行符
  • 我的拼图输入没有
  • 这应该是一个愚蠢的

值得庆幸的是，机器人列表的顺序相同。

例子：

 Blueprint 1: Each ore robot costs 3 ore. Each clay robot costs 3 ore. Each obsidian robot costs 3 ore and 16 clay. Each geode robot costs 3 ore and 20 obsidian.

哦，看起来每行正好有 7 个数字！

哇，这就是一个简单的regex ！

匹配每个数字。

然后我可以将每个解构为变量：

 let [ id, oreRobotOreCost, clayRobotOreCost, obsidianRobotOreCost, obsidianRobotClayCost, geodeRobotOreCost, geodeRobotObsidianCost ] = [...blueprint.matchAll(/\d+/g)] .map(match => +match[0])

我可以使用递归吗？

  • 我需要模拟工厂运行蓝图的 24 分钟
  • 每个蓝图可以生产不同类型的机器人，具体取决于生产的机器人产生了多少矿物
  • 因此，我还需要为每个蓝图播放（全部？）分支，以确定最终产生最多晶洞收集机器人的那个
  • 这对我来说就像递归
  • 是一股臭味
  • 但我必须艰难地度过它，要么足够长的时间生存下来，要么直到我遇到无法逾越的算法障碍

我的递归函数的参数

我想我需要跟踪：

  • 当前分钟
  • 目前收集到的矿物
  • 迄今为止生产的机器人类型和数量

我对矿产需求的传说是在函数始终可以访问的本地范围内。

我将在外部跟踪和更新从已经探索的蓝图场景中可生产的最大数量的晶洞机器人。

接下来，我将如何使用和更新这些参数以成功播放场景？

让条件暴露我的障碍

  • 我开始建立与我收集了多少特定矿物相关的条件
  • 这样做揭示了我的算法存在差距
  • 我不知道如何解释这个差距

差距：

  • 我如何以编程方式告诉我的算法何时构建机器人或何时等待、收集更多矿物并构建不同的机器人？

没有足够的计算机科学技能

对于如何使用算法解决这个问题，我真的感到很困惑。

可悲的是，我不得不空手离开另一个谜题。

今年感觉像是迄今为止最艰难的一年。

原文:

  1. Assembly line feels like assembly code
  2. Building each blueprint
  3. Could I use recursion?
  4. My recursive function's parameters
  5. Letting conditions reveal my blocker
  6. Not Enough Computer Science Skills

I'm not feeling great about this puzzle:

  • Each blueprint requires playing out several scenarios
  • Meaning, underlying all of this is a graph data structure filled with each possible move available in each subsequent minute for each blueprint
  • I'm not well-practiced in graphs
  • I'm still intimidated by recursion and search algorithms

Working in my favor:

  • I understand the problem
  • I know I can parse the list of blueprints into a dictionary of the mineral costs of each robot
  • I sense I could play out a scenario where the moment a robot capable of making a mineral of the next type can be made, my code would make it

The problem comes in the branches where the factory could choose to wait for more resources to make more of a lesser-type robot instead of one that is closer to a geode robot.

Let's see how this goes, and how far I get!

Building each blueprint

  • The example input features convenient line breaks
  • My puzzle input does not
  • This should be a doozy

Thankfully, the list of robots is in the same order.

Example:

Blueprint 1: Each ore robot costs 3 ore. Each clay robot costs 3 ore. Each obsidian robot costs 3 ore and 16 clay. Each geode robot costs 3 ore and 20 obsidian.

Oh, and it looks like each line features exactly 7 numbers!

Wow, then this is a simple regex!

That matches each digit.

I can then de-structure each one as a variable:

let [ id, oreRobotOreCost, clayRobotOreCost, obsidianRobotOreCost, obsidianRobotClayCost, geodeRobotOreCost, geodeRobotObsidianCost
] = [...blueprint.matchAll(/\d+/g)] .map(match => +match[0])

Could I use recursion?

  • I need to simulate 24 minutes of a factory running a blueprint
  • Each blueprint can produce different types of robots depending on how much minerals the produced robots have generated
  • I therefore also need to play out (all?) branches for each blueprint to identify the one that eventually produces the most geode-collecting robots
  • This smells like recursion to me
  • It's a stinky smell
  • But I gotta wade through it either long enough to survive or until I hit an insurmountable algorithmic hurdle

My recursive function's parameters

I think I need to track:

  • The current minute
  • The minerals collected thus far
  • The robot types - and quantity - produced thus far

My legend of mineral requirements is in a local scope that the function can always access.

And I'll externally track and update the largest number of geode robots producible from the already-explored blueprint's scenarios.

Next, how will I use and update these parameters to successfully play out a scenario?

Letting conditions reveal my blocker

  • I started building conditions related to how much of a particular mineral I had collected
  • Doing this revealed a gap in my algorithm
  • I have no idea how to account for that gap

That gap:

  • How could I programmatically tell my algorithm when to build a robot or when to wait, collect more minerals, and build a different robot?

Not Enough Computer Science Skills

I truly feel stumped as to how to solve this problem using an algorithm.

Sadly, I have to turn away empty-handed from another puzzle.

This is feeling like the toughest year of puzzles thus far.
